# pfsense_gateway (Data Source)

Retrieves a single [gateway](https://docs.netgate.com/pfsense/en/latest/routing/gateways.html) by name. Gateways are used by static routes and can be organized into gateway groups for failover and load balancing.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up a single gateway by name
data "pfsense_gateway" "wan" {
  name = "WAN_GW"
}

output "wan_gateway_ip" {
  value = data.pfsense_gateway.wan.gateway
}

output "wan_is_default" {
  value = data.pfsense_gateway.wan.default_gw
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the gateway. Must be unique.

### Read-Only

- `action_disable` (Boolean) Disable any action taken on gateway events. No action will be taken on gateway events but the gateway's status will be tracked.
- `alert_interval` (Number) Alert interval in milliseconds. Defaults to '1000'.
- `data_payload` (Number) ICMP data payload size in bytes. Defaults to '1'.
- `default_gw` (Boolean) Mark this gateway as the default gateway.
- `description` (String) For administrative reference (not parsed).
- `disabled` (Boolean) Disable this gateway.
- `force_down` (Boolean) Mark gateway as down. Forces this gateway to be considered down.
- `gateway` (String) Gateway IP address, or 'dynamic' for DHCP/PPPoE interfaces.
- `interface` (String) Network interface for the gateway.
- `interval` (Number) Probe interval in milliseconds. Defaults to '500'.
- `ipprotocol` (String) IP protocol. Options: `inet`, `inet6`.
- `latency_high` (Number) High latency threshold in milliseconds. Defaults to '500'.
- `latency_low` (Number) Low latency threshold in milliseconds. Defaults to '200'.
- `loss_high` (Number) High packet loss threshold in percent. Defaults to '20'.
- `loss_interval` (Number) Time in milliseconds before packets are treated as lost. Defaults to '2000'.
- `loss_low` (Number) Low packet loss threshold in percent. Defaults to '10'.
- `monitor` (String) Alternative address to monitor link. Use this to monitor an IP address other than the gateway for link status.
- `monitor_disable` (Boolean) Disable gateway monitoring. Consider this gateway as always being up.
- `non_local_gateway` (Boolean) Allow the use of a gateway outside of the interface's subnet.
- `time_period` (Number) Averaging time period in milliseconds for gateway quality calculation. Defaults to '60000'.
- `weight` (Number) Weight for this gateway when used in a gateway group (range 1-30). Defaults to '1'.
